destinations.evidence: The constructor takes one capacity argument, and registry config reads only contract address and max size, not any TTL setting.
A live registry reconstruction call site passes only max_capacity to ExecutorRegistry::new.

equivalence_argument: All observed construction sites use the one-argument capacity constructor; searches found no EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_TTL or Duration argument for ExecutorRegistry::new.
rationale: The primary diff changes ExecutorRegistry::new signatures and call sites from (max_executors, Duration) to max_executors only and removes executor_ttl_seconds config/env parsing. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: All ExecutorRegistry construction sites must provide only max_executors, with no TTL duration or EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_TTL configuration involved.
suggested_falsification_tests: Compile all ExecutorRegistry construction sites and assert no code path reads EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_TTL or passes a Duration to ExecutorRegistry::new.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Compile call sites and grep the port for EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_TTL, executor_ttl_seconds, and any two-argument ExecutorRegistry::new call.
title: ExecutorRegistry constructor takes only capacity
